free_text: Activation cross-sections of deuteron induced nuclear
reactions on neodymium up to 50 MeV
Natural Nd metallic foil 100 um thick and Nd2O3 oxide
powder pressed into Al cup (backing 360 um)and
covered Al foil (10 um). Nd2O3 target thickness: 74.7 -
irradiated in Louvain la Neuve, with deuteron beam
50 MeV, 92 nA for 60 min. Activity of each foil was
measured 4 times. Oxide targets were irradiated in
Sendai, with deuteron beam of 40 MeV, 45 nA for 20 min.
monitor reaction was used in both cases.
The uncertainty on the median energy in the
foils includes the cumulative effects of uncertainty of
incident beam energy, uncertainty of stopping power of
each foil due to thickness variation and straggling
The uncertainty on the cross section values
are estimated in the standard way as the square root
of the quadratic summation of the independently
Fitted excitation functions were used for
